title: cuphea × purpurea
intro: Cuphea × purpurea, also known as the firecracker plant or Mexican heather, is a hybrid plant species in the Lythraceae family, characterized by its small, delicate flowers and shrub-like growth habit. Traditionally, Cuphea × purpurea has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving respiratory conditions, as well as supporting the immune system and providing antioxidant protection. Cuphea × purpurea can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal and health-promoting properties.
